overview.headline: Add loyalty points for checkout: integer accrual from subtotal, burn after discount, and workflow update for Code Beat overview reporting.
overview.body:
## What this PR does

Adds a loyalty points system to the checkout flow. Customers earn points based on subtotal (1 point per whole dollar) and can burn points as a discount (1 point = 1 cent) applied after any percentage discount.

## Big picture

- **Accrual** (`src/loyalty.ts`): `pointsForSubtotal(subtotalCents)` returns `Math.floor(subtotalCents / 100)`. Points are integers, avoiding floating-point drift.
- **Burn** (`src/loyalty.ts`): `centsFromPoints(points)` returns `Math.floor(points)` – a simple 1:1 cent-per-point conversion.
- **Integration** (`src/pricing.ts`): New `applyLoyaltyBurn(subtotal, pointsToBurn)` subtracts burned cents from the subtotal (clamped to 0). It is called **after** `applyDiscount`, so percent discounts are applied first and do not reduce the value of points.
- **Workflow** (`.github/workflows/code-beat.yml`): Updated to use the `improve-pr-overview-report` branch of Code Beat, enabling report generation with an overview. Also configures review/code-quality models, number of runs, and retry logic.

## Major decisions

- **Integer points**: Points are whole numbers, accrued from whole dollars only. Avoids rounding issues from floating-point arithmetic.
- **Burn order**: Loyalty burn is applied after the percent discount. This ensures customers get full value from points regardless of promotional discounts.
- **Simple 1:1 conversion**: 1 point = 1 cent. No tiered burn rates or value multipliers, keeping the system straightforward for the initial implementation.

## Scope

- **In scope**: New loyalty module, integration into existing pricing pipeline, and workflow configuration change to enable the Code Beat overview feature.
- **Out of scope**: User-facing UI, point redemption limits, expiration, tiers, or any database/storage for balances. This is purely the calculation logic.
majorDecisions: Integer points (accrued from whole dollars) to avoid floating-point drift.
Burn applied after percent discount so deals don't reduce point value.
1 point = 1 cent conversion for simplicity; no tiered rates yet.

review.summary: Code Beat did not find clear actionable issues in this pull request.
